description: Missing out-of-memory check when creating a SuppressedError during AsyncDisposableStack disposal (explicit resource management). When an async disposal raises an error while a prior suppressed error already exists, CreateSuppressedError allocates a new SuppressedError object; under OOM the allocation returns null but the result was used without a null check, so js::CreateSuppressedError passed a non-object value into js::NativeDefineDataProperty / js::NativeDefineProperty, which dereferenced it as a NativeObject -> type confusion (debug builds assert isObject() at js/Value.h:972; opt builds SIGSEGV at NativeDefineProperty 'mov (%rcx),%rax'). Regressed by bug 1927195, which enabled the explicit-resource-management build flag on Nightly (feature still pref-gated off by default), so the bug only affected manually-enabled Nightly. Fix: hg c3ab38fe90bf 'Check for out of memory when creating SuppressedError. r=arai' (Phabricator D230733, bug 1934365), which adds the missing OOM/null check. Reported by choller@mozilla.com (fuzzing); patch by debadree333@gmail.com.

fixes.description: Bug 1934365: Check for out of memory when creating SuppressedError. r=arai
